segments.text: The Israelites moved on and camped on the plains of Moab east of the Jordan, opposite Jericho.
Balak, son of Zippor, had seen all that the Israelites had done to the Amorites.
The Moabites were terrified of the Israelites because there were so many of them. The Moabites dreaded the arrival of the Israelites
and told the leaders of Midian, {
This horde will eat up everything we have, just like an ox eats up grass in the field!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: (Balak son of Zippor, was king of Moab at that time.)
He sent messengers to call Balaam, son of Beor, who lived in Pethor near the Euphrates River in his own country. {
Listen, a group of people has arrived here who came from Egypt,
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Balak said in his message to Balaam.
There are hordes of them and they present a real threat to me.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Please come immediately and curse these people for me, because they are stronger than me. Maybe then I'll be able to attack them and drive them out of my country because I know that whoever you bless is blessed, and whoever you curse is cursed.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: The Moabite and Midianite leaders departed, taking payment for the fortune-telling with them. When they arrived they gave Balaam the message from Balak.
Stay the night and I'll let you know the answer the Lord[^37] gives me,
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Balaam told them. So the Moabite leaders stayed there with Balaam.
God came to Balaam and asked him, {
Who are these men staying with you?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Balaam told God, {
Balak, son of Zippor, the king of Moab, sent me this message:
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: ‘Listen, a group of people has arrived here who came from Egypt. There are hordes of them. Please come immediately and curse these people for me. Maybe then I'll be able to fight them and drive them out of my country.’
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: But God told Balaam, {
You are not to go back with them. You must not curse this people, for they are blessed.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In the morning Balaam got up and said to Balak's messengers, {
Go back to where you came from because the Lord has refused to allow me to go with you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The Moabite leaders left. They returned to Balak, and told him, {
Balaam refused to come back with us.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then Balak sent even more leaders who were more prestigious than before.
When they arrived they told Balaam, {
This is what Balak son of Zippor says: ‘Please don't let anything stop you from coming to see me,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: because I will pay you a great deal and follow all the advice you give me. Please come and curse these people for me!’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But Balaam told Balak's officials, {
Even if Balak gave me his whole palace full of silver and gold, I couldn't disobey the command of the Lord my God in any way. [^38]
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Now you should also stay the night so I can see if the Lord has anything else to tell me.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: God came to Balaam during the night and told him, {
Since these men have come for you, get up and go with them. But only do what I tell you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In the morning Balaam got up, put a saddle on his donkey, and left with the Moabite leaders.
God got angry because Balaam had decided to go. The angel of the Lord came and stood in the road to confront him. Balaam was riding on his donkey, and he was accompanied by his two servants.
The donkey saw the angel of the Lord standing in the road holding a drawn sword, so it turned from the road and went into a field. So Balaam beat it to make it go back to the road.
Then the angel of the Lord stood in a narrow part of the road that passed between two vineyards, with walls on both sides.
The donkey saw the angel of the Lord and tried to get past. [^39] It pushed up against the wall and crushed Balaam's foot against it. So he beat it again.
Then the angel of the Lord went ahead and stood in a narrow place where there was no room to get past, either on the right or the left.
The donkey saw the angel of the Lord and lay down under Balaam. He got angry and beat it with his walking stick.
The Lord gave the donkey the ability to speak and it said to Balaam, {
What have I done to you for you to beat me three times?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: You made me look stupid!
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Balaam told the donkey.
If I was holding a sword, I'd kill you now!
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: But the donkey asked Balaam, {
Aren't I the donkey you've ridden all your life right up to today? Have I ever treated you this way before?
segments.speaker: The donkey

segments.text: No,
segments.speaker: The donkey

segments.text: he admitted.
Then the Lord gave Balaam the ability to see the angel of the Lord standing in the road holding a drawn sword. Balaam bowed low and fell facedown on the ground.
The angel of the Lord asked him, {
Why did you beat your donkey three times? Listen, I have come to confront you because you're being obstinate.
segments.speaker: the Angel of the Lord

segments.text: The donkey saw me and avoided me three times. If it had not avoided me, by now I definitely would have killed you and allowed the donkey to live.
segments.speaker: the Angel of the Lord

segments.text: I have sinned because I did not realize that you were standing in the road to confront me,
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Balaam said to the angel of the Lord,
So if this isn't what you want, I'll go back home.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: The angel of the Lord told Balaam, {
No, you can go with the men, but only say what I tell you.
segments.speaker: the Angel of the Lord

segments.text: So Balaam continued on with Balak's officials.
When Balak found out that Balaam was on his way, he went out to meet him at the Moabite town on the border at the Arnon River, the farthest point of his territory.
He said to Balaam, {
Didn't you think my call for you to come was urgent? Why didn't you come to me straight away? Did you think I couldn't pay you enough?
segments.speaker: the Angel of the Lord

segments.text: Look, I'm here with you now, aren't I?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Balaam replied.
But do you think I can just say anything? I can only speak the words that God gives me to say.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: So Balaam went with Balak and they arrived at Kiriath-huzoth.
Balak sacrificed cattle and sheep, and he shared the meat with Balaam and the leaders who were with him.
The next morning Balak took Balaam up to Bamoth-baal. [^40] From there he could see the extent of the Israelite camp.
